Title: Work-based learning funding raised to RM9.9mil, recognising armed forces personnel experience
Article:
MELAKA: Allocation for the Work-Based Learning (WBL) programme for the armed forces has been increased from RM3 million to RM9.9 million this year, expanding opportunities for 1,520 personnel to obtain academic recognition based on their experience and skills gained throughout their service.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the strategic initiative is aimed at improving the employability of armed forces personnel after service, while helping them secure more stable jobs and income.
"So far, a total of 461 armed forces personnel have benefited from the WBL programme, with 231 enrolled at Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) and 230 at Universiti Pertahanan Nasional Malaysia (UPNM), and all are expected to graduate in November and December this year.
"For this year, the programme has been expanded to 12 higher education institutions, with nearly 50 study programmes offered across various fields, including business administration, culinary arts, defence technology and logistics management," he said.
He spoke to reporters after the launch of the Defence Ministry's WBL Programme at Terendak Camp here today.
Also present were Royal Malaysian Navy Chief Admiral Tan Sri Dr Zulhelmy Ith Nain and Armed Forces Headquarters Chief of Staff Lieutenant-General Datuk Seri Muhammad Huzaimi Sabri.
Earlier, Mohamed Khaled witnessed the exchange of Memoranda of Agreement between the armed forces, UPNM, UiTM and City University Malaysia, symbolising stronger strategic cooperation in implementing the WBL initiative.
He also presented certificates of completion to 36 participants of Cohort 1/2025 who began their studies in June 2025, as well as offer letters to 24 new intakes for Cohort 2/2026 who will begin their studies next month.
He said the programme is important as most military personnel join the service with only the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) qualification, adding that without intervention, they would retire with the same qualification in an increasingly competitive job market.
"Most military personnel enter the service with only SPM qualifications, while over their roughly 20 years of service they accumulate experience, skills and competencies, but these are often not recognised by industry without formal certification," he said.
He said that through collaboration with higher education institutions, personnel's experience and skills will be assessed and recognised, allowing them to obtain diplomas or formal academic qualifications through a work-based learning approach.
"With better academic qualifications, it will help armed forces personnel, who on average retire at around 41 and still have growing families to support, to secure jobs and reduce the risk of financial hardship if they rely only on pensions and welfare assistance.
"At the same time, this work-based learning approach does not interfere with or affect their core duties in service, an advantage that personnel should make full use of," he added.
–Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iris issued UMA query as stock surges 66.7pct
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia has issued an unusual market activity (UMA) query to Iris Group Bhd following a sharp rise in the company's share price.
At the time of writing, Iris Group's shares had surged 66.67 per cent, or 12 sen, to 30 sen, making it the second most active counter on Bursa Malaysia.
The stock opened at 18.5 sen, up from its previous close of 18 sen, and climbed to an intraday high of 33 sen.
A total of 59.78 million shares changed hands, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM244.71 million.
The exchange has requested the Iris group clarify whether there are any undisclosed corporate developments relating to the group's business and affairs that may have contributed to the surge in its share price and trading activity, according to its filing.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jobless rate at decade low, youth unemployment remains high
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The national unemployment rate remained at its lowest level in a decade in March, but young people are still facing significant challenges in securing jobs, particularly those newly entering the labour market.
MBSB Research, BIMB Securities Sdn Bhd and TA Securities confirmed that the country's overall unemployment rate remained stable at 2.9 per cent.
This marks the fifth consecutive month that Malaysia's unemployment rate has stayed below 3.0 per cent, a level typically regarded as full employment.
However, analysis reports show that unemployment among youth aged 15 to 24 remains high at a double-digit rate of 10.2 per cent.
This figure represents about 290,000 young people who are still unemployed and have yet to secure jobs.
MBSB Research said the persistently high youth unemployment rate signals that the transition process for graduates and new jobseekers into the labour market is still not running smoothly.
According to the firm, the situation "continues to reflect a disrupted transition phase for those trying to enter the workforce for the first time".
This suggests that current economic growth is still not broad-based enough to effectively absorb new graduates and young workers.
At the same time, BIMB Securities also noted that job creation growth is currently at its slowest pace since March 2021.
This development raises concerns about the labour market's ability to continue generating sufficient employment opportunities, particularly heading into the second half of 2026.
Economists also expect hiring conditions to become more subdued in the coming months due to global economic uncertainty and more cautious hiring by companies.
As a result, economic observers believe that corporations and industries need to strengthen their hiring and talent development strategies to ensure that young people are not further left behind in the labour market.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suspected mastermind in nude assault, robbery remanded, hunt on for two more
Article:
BATU PAHAT: An unemployed man believed to be the mastermind behind the robbery and assault of a woman who was allegedly forced to strip naked and recorded on video was remanded for six days by the magistrate's court here today.
Magistrate Nurasidah A. Rahman issued the remand order against the 32-year-old suspect and another man, 46, until Wednesday to facilitate investigations.
The main suspect, who has 12 priors involving drug and criminal offences, was arrested with his accomplice at 1.30am after the vehicle they were in skidded while being pursued by police.
The arrests were made by a team from the Special Investigation/Serious Crimes Division (D9) after police received information on the suspects' whereabouts in Kulai.
The suspects sped off after noticing the cops but managed to flee only a few kilometres before their car skidded.
Police also recovered a Glock 17 pistol from the vehicle.
In the 7am incident on Thursday, the suspect and two women, who had earlier been remanded, allegedly robbed and confined a 32-year-old woman at a house in Taman Banang Heights here.
The suspects allegedly assaulted the victim, burned her ankle with a lighter and forced her to strip before recording her in the nude.
The victim was also repeatedly punched in the body and face, causing injuries.
Police are investigating the case under Sections 395, 342, 324 and 509 of the Penal Code.
Police are tracking down two more suspects, a man and a woman, believed to be involved in the incident.
The victim reportedly lost more than RM5,000 and a mobile phone.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DAP leaders urged to end infighting, uphold unity
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: DAP veteran and former Penang executive councillor Datuk Seri Phee Boon Poh has called on party leaders to uphold stability, discipline and collective responsibility amid ongoing internal tensions involving the Penang state government.
In a statement today, Phee said the people expected the party to provide "stability, mature leadership and responsible governance" instead of continuous public attacks against its own party-led administration.
He said differences in views and constructive criticism were normal in any democratic party, but stressed that there was a distinction between constructive feedback and actions that created prolonged public pressure and political tension against the government and the chief minister.
Phee said Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow had shown "considerable patience, restraint and professionalism" in leading the state government despite repeated internal provocations over the years.
"If there were genuine concerns regarding government matters, such issues should first be raised through appropriate internal discussions and party-government channels," he said.
He did not refer to any specific incident.
However, the latest was yesterday's war of words at the state assembly sitting between Chow and his predecessor Lim Guan Eng over the Batu Kawan Industrial Park 2 (BKIP2) project.
The exchange began when
Chow defended the state government's decision to reject an RM818 million bid
for the BKIP2 project in Ladang Byram by a consortium comprising IJM Corporation and Aspen Group.
Prior to that, Lim had also attacked Chow on numerous occasions over the hike in quit rent.
Phee said party discipline and collective responsibility must always be upheld, especially among senior leaders with extensive experience in government and party affairs.
He said grassroots members and the public found it difficult to understand why certain matters were repeatedly raised publicly and in the state assembly instead of being discussed internally through established channels.
"The rakyat want to see leaders solving problems together, not engaging in prolonged public political sparring that ultimately weakens public confidence in the government and creates confusion among grassroots supporters," he added.
He said no individual was bigger than the party and that political renewal was necessary to allow younger leaders to gain experience and assume greater responsibilities.
He said many younger DAP leaders were already carrying major responsibilities in government and at the grassroots level under increasingly difficult political and economic conditions.
"They should be guided and strengthened, not constantly subjected to internal public attacks and political manoeuvring," he said.
Phee further urged DAP leaders to demonstrate maturity and unity at a time when external political forces were attempting to weaken both the Unity Government and Penang's stability.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Azmin appointed as Bersatu election director
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu has agreed to appoint former Selangor menteri besar Datuk Seri Mohamed Azmin Ali as the party's election director ahead of possible state polls.
Azmin, who is also Bersatu secretary-general, said the decision was made at the party's supreme council meeting yesterday in place of recent developments in Negri Sembilan and Melaka, which are expected to face state elections in the near future.
"The party has made preparations for the upcoming elections through the establishment of a main operations centre. This facility is equipped with advanced modern technology and a digital election operating system using the SIBER platform, which will begin operations on June 1," he said in a statement.
Azmin said that next month, the council will hold a high-level strategic discussion involving top leadership from Johor, Negri Sembilan, and Melaka to refine new strategies for generating public support and championing their shared agenda.
He said the supreme council meeting, chaired by Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin, lasting nearly four hours, also focused on current political developments in Johor.
"The supreme council received a briefing from Johor Bersatu chief and resolved to instruct the state leadership to enhance discussions and cooperation with Johor Perikatan Nasional (PN) and other parties ready to bring change to the state."
He added that audits, monitoring and utilising the NADI application, led by Datuk Seri Ahmad Faizal Azumu, have been mobilised extensively across areas that are the focus of Bersatu.
Azmin said that he also supported Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz to boost programmes and strategic communication via traditional and social media.
"Bersatu remains confident that harmony and unity within PN will instil confidence in the people to stand with the coalition in the upcoming elections.
He also said the supreme council has agreed to appoint Bersatu Armada chief Muhammad Hilman Idham as the party's representative in the PN supreme council.
"As a prominent figure and a young leader, he will bring a sense of freshness and intelligence to the PN leadership," he said.
He added that the council also reviewed the report of the special task force committee for Division Restructuring and agreed to confirm the appointments of Datuk Seri Abdul Azim Mohd Zabidi as the Lembah Pantai Division chief and Datuk Safian Mohd Yunus as the Bandar Tun Razak Division chief.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Interest-driven travel trends open new opportunities for Southeast Asian countries
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Changing consumer behaviour and interest-driven travel trends are reshaping the regional tourism landscape, presenting new opportunities for countries in Southeast Asia, including Malaysia, to attract a growing segment of experience-focused travellers.
This shift is expected to benefit regional tourism markets which have been actively positioning themselves as a hub for ecotourism, wellness tourism, food tourism, luxury hospitality and cultural experiences amid the broader recovery in international travel.
Industry participants at the recent Xiaohongshu Business x Singapore Outbound Tourism Forum noted that tourism demand is increasingly being shaped by lifestyle interests, cultural experiences, wellness activities, sporting events and local immersion rather than traditional destination-driven travel.
According to Xiaohongshu's data covering January to December 2025, the platform recorded 130 million monthly active outbound travel users, with more than 90 per cent actively conducting travel-related searches.
Tim Zhang, general manager of Xiaohongshu Commercial Cross-border & Internet Industry Group, said modern travellers are increasingly pursuing journeys aligned with personal interests and emotional experiences rather than purely destination-based itineraries.
He noted that the rise of "blind box-style travel" is being driven by consumers seeking emotional resonance, curiosity for new experiences and deeper engagement with local lifestyle aesthetics.
Analysts said the trend could create significant opportunities for Malaysia's tourism sector, particularly as travellers increasingly seek authentic cultural experiences, premium wellness offerings, nature-based tourism and experiential urban travel across Southeast Asia.
Malaysia's strong connectivity, diverse tourism assets and growing digital economy ecosystem place the country in a favourable position to capture demand from Chinese outbound travellers and younger regional consumers seeking highly personalised travel experiences.
Meanwhile, Kwong Dodo, regional head of Xiaohongshu Commercial Cross-border Asia & Middle East, highlighted broader changes in outbound travel behaviour across Asia-Pacific markets and key evolutions for Singapore's outbound travel.
"As one of the destinations in the APAC region with regular top-tier sports events and flagship live performances, Singapore boasts strong re-visit appeal," he said.
He noted that destinations with strong lifestyle appeal, premium service experiences and recurring international events are seeing higher revisit potential and stronger engagement among younger travellers.
Industry observers said Malaysia could benefit from similar trends through its expanding calendar of international sporting events, concerts, cultural festivals, wellness retreats and luxury tourism developments.
Xiaohongshu's consumer insights also identified several fast-growing travel demographics, including young urban consumers travelling with parents, digitally-driven travellers aged between 19 and 25, middle-income groups focused on lifestyle experiences and a new generation of parents seeking child-friendly and educational travel experiences.
The findings suggest that tourism operators will need to develop more personalised digital content, targeted tourism offerings and immersive travel experiences to remain competitive in the evolving regional tourism market.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MN Holdings bags RM83.5mil data centre infrastructure contract
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MN Holdings Bhd has secured an RM83.5 million contract to build a new 275 kilovolt (kV) consumer landing station (CLS) for a data centre in the southern region of Peninsular Malaysia.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said its wholly-owned subsidiary MN Utilities Engineering Sdn Bhd accepted the letter of award on May 15 from a customer involved in infrastructure hosting, data processing services and related activities.
The scope of works includes the design, construction, supply, delivery, installation, testing and commissioning of the CLS.
The contract, valued at RM83.5 million excluding sales and service tax, will commence on May 11, 2026 and is expected to be completed by Jan 31, 2027.
MN Holdings said the contract is expected to contribute positively to the group's future earnings and net assets per share upon commencement of the project.
The company added that the award is not expected to have any effect on its issued share capital or substantial shareholders' shareholding structure.
At the time of writing, MN Holdings' shares were up one sen or 0.43 per cent to RM2.31, giving it a market capitalisation of RM1.53 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cops bust international scam syndicate, seize RM57.68mil in assets, arrest 187
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have crippled an international online fraud syndicate after arresting 187 people and seizing RM57.68 million in assets in a nationwide operation earlier this month.
Inspector-General of Police Datuk Seri Mohd Khalid Ismail said Op Teguh 2.0, from May 6-7, involved 46 raids on homes, including luxury condominiums in the Klang Valley.
The operation was carried out with the Criminal Investigation Department, the federal police Anti-Money Laundering Investigation Division, Special Branch, CyberSecurity Malaysia and Telekom Malaysia.
A total of 187 suspects – 151 men and 36 women of various nationalities – were detained for their involvement in cross-border online fraud activities.
"The arrests involved 129 Chinese nationals, 23 Malaysians, nine Japanese, eight Vietnamese, seven Indonesians, four Laotians, three Thais, two Filipinos and two Myanmar.
"Police seized syndicate assets worth RM57.68 million, including three luxury properties valued at RM38 million.
"Police also impounded 20 luxury vehicles worth RM6.58 million, including a Rolls-Royce Cullinan, Mercedes-Benz G63, Toyota Supra MK4 and Zeekr 009 Executive," he said at a press conference in Bukit Aman.
Khalid said police also confiscated 67 luxury items, including watches, handbags, jewellery, wallets and gold bars worth RM12 million.
Authorities also seized 556 digital devices valued at RM552,000, as well as cash, foreign currency, debit and credit cards, and cryptocurrency assets totalling RM549,786.
"To date, 24 investigation papers have been opened," he said.
Investigations revealed the syndicate targeted victims in Hong Kong, China, South Korea and Japan.
"Among the modus operandi were fake investment scams using fraudulent investment platforms targeting victims in Hong Kong and China.
"In phone scams, syndicates impersonated Japanese police officers via the BRIA Mobile application, Telegram and Line, while love scams involved deceiving victims through online relationships, mainly targeting South Korean and Japanese nationals," he said.
Khalid said online gambling scams involved fake betting applications and illegal financial transactions targeting victims from Hong Kong and China.
He said investigations are ongoing to identify the syndicate's full network, including masterminds, financial controllers and cross-border criminal links.
"Three people – two Chinese and a local – are being sought to assist investigations," he said.
Khalid said preliminary investigations found the foreign suspects had valid travel documents to enter Malaysia, adding that the syndicates were operating with local help.
"Some entered the country as tourists. Police and the Immigration Department are working closely to monitor the situation.
"This is not a new initiative. Op Teguh 1.0 last year also took similar steps," he said.
He added that police are also working with the Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry (MOTAC) on the matter.
"The case involves only a small number of them (foreigners)," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke puzzled by opposition to UEC pathway into public universities
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said he was puzzled by opposition from certain quarters to the recognition of the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) for admission into public institutions of higher learning.
He said the move would expand access to education.
"We are expanding access for students to pursue higher education, and that is a good thing. I am puzzled as to why certain quarters are opposing it," he told reporters after launching the Segambut Utara KTM station today.
It was previously reported that Gabungan Pelajar Melayu Semenanjung (GPMS) Perak had opposed the Higher Education Ministry's recognition of the UEC.
Earlier, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government would
further refine details of its decision
allowing school leavers from tahfiz institutions and Chinese independent schools (SMPC), including those holding the UEC, to pursue selected programmes at public universities.
Anwar said he was confident the move would be accepted by all parties, as UEC students would still be required to pass Bahasa Melayu and History at Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) level.
"This is not only for UEC holders. Tahfiz certificate holders are also given the same pathway.
"We want to improve access to education and ensure our children receive better education.
"But certain quarters are objecting for the sake of objecting and want to deny our children the opportunity to further their studies," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara: Inflation outlook hinges on global energy and commodity price movements
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's inflation outlook will continue to depend largely on external cost developments, particularly fluctuations in global energy and commodity prices.
Bank Negara Malaysia governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said headline inflation is projected to average between 1.5 per cent and 2.5 per cent in 2026 and may trend closer to the upper end of the range.
"In line with expectations, inflation is expected to edge higher, mainly reflecting higher global energy prices and related cost pressures following the escalation of the Middle East conflict," he said at a press conference on Malaysia's first quarter (Q1) 2026 gross domestic product (GDP) performance today.
Headline inflation rose to 1.6 per cent during Q1 2026, in part reflecting the initial passthrough of higher global cost pressures.
Abdul Rasheed said the surge in global energy prices led to higher domestic fuel prices in March, namely for RON97 and unsubsidised diesel, contributing to the increase in headline inflation.
"Nevertheless, this was partly offset by lower core inflation, which moderated to 2.1 per cent, suggesting that underlying inflation remained moderate amid steady demand conditions," he noted.
At the same time, Abdul Rasheed said weaker global demand and trade activity could weigh on domestic demand conditions, including discretionary spending.
He noted that softer global commodity prices and easing imported cost pressures could also contribute to lower inflation outcomes.
"In the absence of excessive demand pressures, current targeted measures, including existing fuel subsidy mechanisms and other mitigation measures, are expected to continue to help limit broader spillovers to domestic inflation in the near term," he said.
However, Abdul Rasheed said the extent of pass-through to domestic prices will also depend on several factors, including firms' pricing behaviour and prevailing demand conditions.
"In this environment, we remain vigilant by tracking these developments closely and will continue to assess how evolving cost and demand conditions affect the inflation outlook," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] No corruption found in KL flood retention pond land probe – MACC
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has found no evidence of corruption in the change of land status involving several Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) flood retention ponds.
However, the anti-graft agency said it had advised DBKL to tighten monitoring and governance of development projects near retention ponds to ensure all conditions and specifications are strictly complied with by contractors.
MACC senior investigations director Datuk Mohd Hafaz Nazar said the case had been classified as "No Further Action" (NFA) following investigations into alleged abuse of power involving several DBKL and Department of Irrigation and Drainage (DID) officers between 2015 and 2021.
"In 2022, the MACC opened an investigation paper following complaints regarding allegations of abuse of power involving several officers from Kuala Lumpur City Hall and the Kuala Lumpur Department of Irrigation and Drainage for the period between 2015 and 2021.
"Among the allegations investigated were the change in status of several areas near water retention ponds in the capital, including in Taman Wahyu, for development purposes.
"The MACC conducted a comprehensive investigation based on documents and statements obtained before the investigation paper was completed in 2023 and referred to the Deputy Public Prosecutor.
"However, based on the findings of the investigation, no elements of corruption were identified, and it was decided that the case be classified as No Further Action (NFA)," he said in a statement today.
The MACC added that any violation of terms affecting the function of the retention ponds falls under DBKL's jurisdiction for further action.
The statement came after Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said she had
yet to receive an update from the MACC
regarding alleged irregularities linked to the alienation of the Batu and Jinjang flood retention ponds.
Yeoh had personally lodged a report with the anti-graft agency in 2021.
Earlier this week, she said the capacity of the critical flood retention ponds in Jinjang and Batu
had shrunk by an estimated 70 per cent
after 17 land lots within the water body zones were transferred to developers beginning in 2015.
Former Federal Territories minister Datuk Seri Tengku Adnan Tengku Mansoor had
denied any knowledge of the transfer
of flood retention pond land to developers during his tenure, saying land matters were outside his ministry's purview at the time.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar increases Malaysian Youth Council allocation to RM5mil
Article:
SEREMBAN: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced a RM5 million allocation for the Malaysian Youth Council (MYC) to strengthen the umbrella body's role in advancing youth development nationwide.
He said the increase of RM1 million from last year's allocation reflected the government's continued commitment to supporting youth development programmes and recognised MBM's role in driving the national youth agenda under the Youth and Sports Ministry.
"MBM is the main youth body under the ministry. So this year, I have agreed to increase the allocation to RM5 million from RM4 million last year," he said when officiating the 2026 National Youth Day celebration here.
Also present were Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun and Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari.
Meanwhile, Anwar said the government had also allocated funds under the Islamic Development Programme benefiting 7,091 Kelas Al-Quran dan Fardu Ain (Kafa) teachers, imams, takmir teachers and mosque personnel nationwide, involving a total allocation of RM42.3 million.
He said support would also be extended to Islamic educational institutions such as pondok schools, tahfiz centres, religious schools and Kafa premises operating outside the supervision of the Education Ministry, with RM5.4 million allocated for maintenance and institutional strengthening.
He added that three projects in Negri Sembilan had been approved under the Prime Minister's Department Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU), involving RM800,000 for minor repair works at Maahad Tahfiz Al-Quran Al-Falah Kuala Sawah Rantau, Madrasah Az-Zaakiriin and Pondok Pesantren Darul Tauhid.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Court grants more time for mental evaluation of Sabah murder accused
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The magistrate's court here today granted a one-month extension for the psychiatric evaluation of a septuagenarian charged with murdering a woman whose dismembered body parts were found in Indah Permai, Sepanggar.
Magistrate Dzul Elmy Yunus made the decision after being informed by the prosecution that the psychiatric report on the accused, Piluta Samad, 71, was not yet ready and that he required a further month of observation at Hospital Mesra Bukit Padang.
There was no objection from Piluta's legal team led by lawyer Datuk Ram Singh. Ram said he hoped an application to transfer the case to the High Court could be made at the next mention date.
The court fixed June 25 for further mention pending completion of the mental evaluation report from Hospital Mesra Bukit Padang.
Piluta was not brought to court during today's proceedings.
Lawyers Joan Goh, Ian Vun and Prem Elmer Ganasan are also representing the accused.
Prosecuting officer Inspector Mohamad Hamidi Mohamad Hamzah appeared for the prosecution on behalf of deputy public prosecutor Kareena Kaur Gill Karamjit Singh.
On April 15, the court ordered Piluta to
undergo 30 days of observation
at Hospital Mesra following an application by the defence to refer him for psychiatric evaluation under Section 342 of the Criminal Procedure Code.
Piluta was charged on Feb 27 with the murder of Hamidah Husin, also known as Lisa, 44.
The alleged offence took place between 6pm and 7pm on Feb 11 at an unnumbered house in Kampung Sri Kenangan, Jalan Sepanggar.
He was charged under Section 302 of the Penal Code, which carries the death penalty or imprisonment of between 30 and 40 years and, if not sentenced to death, a minimum of 12 strokes of the rotan, upon conviction.
No plea has been recorded as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court.
It was previously reported that the victim's body had allegedly been dismembered and disposed of at several rubbish sites. Two body parts – the left leg and right hand – have yet to be recovered.
Piluta, believed to be the victim's former fiancé, was arrested as the main suspect. Three others – his children, comprising one son and two daughters – were also detained to assist in the investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teacher bags RM1mil BSN SSP jackpot ahead of Teachers' Day
Article:
IPOH: A secondary school History teacher from Butterworth has turned this year's Teachers' Day into a life-changing moment after winning RM1 million in the Bank Simpanan Nasional (BSN) Sijil Simpanan Premium (SSP) monthly millionaire draw.
M. Munisuaran, 56, was named the 123rd winner, receiving the windfall just days before the national Teachers' Day celebration.
He received news of the win on May 2 from a close friend, who is also a corporate manager at BSN.
"I usually follow the live monthly draws. I had always hoped for something like a car, but I never expected anything like this," he said when met today. He was accompanied by his wife, G. Manjula, 55, who is also a teacher at the same school.
Munisuaran, who is from Parit Buntar, said this was his first win through the SSP scheme, although his children had previously enjoyed rewards under the junior savings category, including an iPad Air.
He received a mock cheque for RM1 million from acting BSN chief executive Mujibburrahman Abd Rashid at a hotel here.
Despite becoming a millionaire overnight, Munisuaran said he remains committed to his long-standing savings discipline.
"I treat SSP as a proper savings plan. Even if I don't win, my savings remain intact.
He said he opened his BSN account in 1990 and began actively contributing to the SSP scheme about 11 years ago, gradually extending the savings habit to his wife and children as part of their education planning.
A father of four, he said the family's savings approach helped support his children through university, covering tuition fees and daily expenses.
"Three of them are already working now, but they still continue saving," he said.
Munisuaran has no immediate plans for the RM1 million prize and will defer any major financial decisions until his retirement in 2030.
"For now, I am still in service. I will plan properly after I retire," he said.
Meanwhile, Mujibburrahman, in a statement, said BSN has contributed more than RM500,000 in conjunction with the 55th National Teachers' Day celebration.
He said the contribution is part of efforts to promote a savings culture and financial literacy among educators, with award recipients also receiving BSN SSP prizes.
Top honours, including the National Teacher Icon and National Education Leadership Icon awards, will receive RM60,000 each in SSP savings.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pahang to meet ministry next week over river sand exports
Article:
KUANTAN: The Pahang government will meet the Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Ministry next week to discuss issues related to river sand exports in greater detail.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail said the matter must be studied comprehensively to safeguard the state's interests, public welfare and revenue.
He said the federal government's restrictions on river sand export permits had directly affected sand industry operations in Pahang, particularly in downstream and estuary areas that require regular dredging.
"Dredging activities in downstream and estuary areas should not be confused with upstream river excavation, as both involve different functions and environmental implications.
"Sand deposits in downstream and estuary zones are largely caused by natural sedimentation resulting from erosion, river currents and water flow," he said in his winding-up speech at the Pahang state assembly sitting today.
Wan Rosdy said the accumulation of sand deposits over time caused riverbeds to become shallow, reducing water flow capacity and increasing flood risks, especially during the monsoon season and periods of heavy rain.
"River dredging in certain areas should not be viewed solely as an economic or sand mining activity, but as an important flood mitigation and river management measure," he said.
He added that without a controlled sand concession mechanism, the state would have to bear substantial costs using public funds for river maintenance, sediment removal and flood mitigation works.
Wan Rosdy said the issue should not be seen purely from a commercial profit perspective, but understood within a broader context involving environmental management, flood mitigation, economic opportunities and state revenue.
Last week, the Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah Al-Sultan Abdullah, expressed disappointment over the federal government's move to
ban river sand exports
.
A day later, Wan Rosdy said the state was
seeking federal approval
to resume river sand exports to strengthen state revenue.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: AirAsia X's stock drops amid Q1 loss
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: AirAsia X Bhd is the most actively traded stock on Bursa Malaysia after it logged losses of RM154.9 million in the first quarter.
At midday break, the counter dropped 4.1 per cent or half a sen to RM1.17, with more than 67 million shares traded.
The stock opened flat on Friday at RM1.21 versus Thursday's close at RM1.22 and dropped to an intraday low of RM1.15.
On a year-to-date basis, the stock has dropped 32.76 per cent.
The company's earnings were affected by net foreign exchange losses following the depreciation of regional currencies.
According to a bourse filing, the low-cost carrier incurred RM232.2 million in foreign exchange losses.
However, it reported a revenue of RM5.9 billion for the quarter, while earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation stood at RM1 billion.
Additionally, analysts  lowered their earnings forecasts and target prices for AirAsia X.
Public Investment Bank Bhd cut its financial year 2026 to 2028 (FY26-FY28) forecasts for the company by an average of 28 per cent and downgraded the stock to "Neutral" with a lower target price of RM1.29 from RM1.85.
Meanwhile, Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) lowered its FY26 earnings forecast to RM331 million from RM1.4 billion, and FY27 estimates to RM1 billion from RM1.4 billion, factoring in lower capacity and higher jet fuel costs.
HLIB maintained its "Buy" call on AirAsia X but lowered its target price to RM2.20 from RM3.35.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: A-GC and MACC 'inseparable', says new chief commissioner Abdul Halim
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Newly appointed Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman paid Attorney-General Tan Sri Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar a courtesy visit yesterday, marking his first official engagement since taking the helm.
"I will do my best to lead, reform, and strengthen the MACC," Abdul Halim said, adding that the Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) and the anti-graft agency are "inseparable".
"The cooperation that has been established will continue."
Abdul Halim said that their work together involves large-scale, high-profile, and public-interest cases, which will continue to be referred to the A-GC as the prosecuting authority responsible for deciding legal action.
Also present during the meeting were A-GC Trial and Appeals Division chief Datuk Seri Saiful Edris Zainuddin and Prosecution Division chief Datuk Masri Mohd Daud.
Abdul Halim took over
as chief commissioner on May 13, succeeding Tan Sri Azam Baki, who ended his tenure on May 12 after leading the commission since 2020.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gobind: Malaysia must go beyond investments to become AI powerhouse, attract global firms
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's ambition to become a regional artificial intelligence (AI) powerhouse cannot be achieved through investment attraction alone but requires a fundamental redesign of how government and industry operate, said Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo.
He said the next phase of growth must move beyond infrastructure build-up and focus on transforming institutional frameworks into systems driven by intelligence, data integration and cross-sector collaboration.
"Humans today are not just users of technology. They're becoming part of the infrastructure that enables businesses to grow," Deo said at the closing of The Asian Banker Summit 2026 here.
Gobind's remarks come as Malaysia continues to position itself as a key digital investment destination, having secured RM144.4 billion (US$30 billion) in data centre and cloud investments between 2021 and June 2025. The broader digital investment pipeline has surpassed RM232.8 billion (US$59 billion) as of April 2025.
Global technology leaders including Microsoft, Google, Amazon Web Services (AWS), Oracle and Nvidia are among those expanding their footprint in Malaysia, reinforcing the country's growing relevance in the global digital supply chain.
The digital economy accounted for about 23 per cent of Malaysia's gross domestic product (GDP) in 2023, with the government targeting a 30 per cent contribution by 2030 under the AI Nation 2030 vision.
However, Gobind stressed that capital inflows alone are not the end goal, but rather a foundation for broader structural transformation.
The key test, he said, is whether these investments translate into domestic capability-building, stronger small and medium enterprises (SMEs), high-value job creation and more inclusive access to financial and digital services.
Malaysia's AI readiness strategy is anchored on four key pillars – connectivity, compute infrastructure, cybersecurity and talent development.
Connectivity infrastructure is largely in place, with 4G coverage reaching 95 to 96 per cent of populated areas and 5G coverage approaching 90 per cent nationwide.
On the regulatory front, the Cybersecurity Act 2024 has been introduced to strengthen protections around national critical information infrastructure, while the Data Sharing Act 2025 is designed to enable secure data exchange across government agencies, supporting more integrated digital services.
A key policy shift is the upcoming Government Innovation Initiative (GII), set to launch on May 25, which will move away from traditional request-for-proposal (RFP) models. Instead, ministries will publish problem statements, share relevant data sets and invite industry players to co-develop solutions, including identifying new use cases from shared data.
Gobind issued a direct challenge to industry stakeholders: "Are you prepared to take that step? The radical step, that total shift in how you operate?"
The Asian Banker Summit 2026 brought together senior banking executives, policymakers, regulators and technology leaders to discuss the future of financial services and the evolving global digital economy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No need for panic buying, rice supply stable until year-end – Mat Sabu
Article:
KLANG: There is no need for panic buying as the country's rice supply is sufficient and expected to remain stable until the end of the year, says Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu.
"Rice supplies are currently stable and are expected to remain so until the end of this year.
"However, this does not mean rice will become unavailable from Jan 1. As we approach the new year, the government will continue taking measures to ensure supply stability," he told reporters after launching the Tuaian Nanas Projek Rintis Pintar for the Kota Raja parliamentary constituency today.
He urged the public to continue purchasing essentials as usual and avoid excessive buying.
"There is no need to buy excessively. Purchase as normal," he said.
Earlier in his speech, Mohamad commended Malaysians for not engaging in panic buying despite global uncertainties linked to the situation in the Middle East.
"Alhamdulillah, thanks to the cooperation of the people, there has been no panic buying in Malaysia.
"People are buying rice, chicken, eggs and vegetables according to their needs, and there is no hoarding," he said.
Mohamad warned that panic buying or excessive purchases would only disrupt supply chains and create unnecessary shortages.
He said the government would continue taking proactive measures to address uncertainties arising from the global situation, including through weekly meetings of the National Economic Action Council.
"Experts in relevant fields will continue to be called by the prime minister to provide their views so we can obtain the latest information and plan accordingly," he said.
He added that continuous monitoring was also being carried out to ensure food supply and price stability.
Meanwhile, Mohamad said applications for special permits to purchase diesel for farmers would be opened at four locations in Northwest Selangor from May 19 to 21 to assist padi farmers and machinery operators, particularly in Sekinchan.
He said while the current allocation stood at 200 litres, some farmers had requested larger quantities.
"For requests exceeding 200 litres, the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry may consider them as the matter falls under its jurisdiction," he said.
Mohamad said applications would be assessed on a case-by-case basis to ensure only genuine recipients received the subsidy and to prevent leakages.
"Authorities have detected subsidy leakages on quite a large scale, which has surprised us.
"It involves misuse of the subsidies currently being provided," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman loses RM245,000 to fake govt tender scam
Article:
MIRI: A businesswoman lost RM245,500 after being duped by a fake government tender for the supply of electronic goods.
Miri police chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said the 61-year-old victim was approached by a suspect posing as a government agency officer.
The victim, who is in the construction industry, was offered a tender to source electronic items and was given a second phone number to arrange the procurement.
"The victim was deceived into making four transactions to a third-party bank account provided by the suspect, involving a total loss of RM245,500," he said in a statement.
The woman only realised she had been cheated after checking with the authorities and discovering the tender did not exist. She subsequently lodged a police report.
The case is being investigated under Section 420 of the Penal Code for cheating, which carries a jail term of between one and 10 years, whipping, and a fine upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Maxis posts higher earnings in Q1 on service revenue growth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Maxis Bhd's net profit rose 12.4 per cent to RM417 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26) from RM371 million a year ago, supported by steady service revenue growth.
Revenue for the quarter climbed 4.7 per cent to RM2.73 billion from RM2.61 billion previously, according to the telco's filing with Bursa Malaysia.
Earnings per share increased to 5.30 sen from 4.70 sen in Q1 FY25
The group declared an interim dividend of four sen per share, unchanged from the corresponding period last year, payable on June 23.
By segment, service revenue grew 3.5 per cent year-on-year to RM1.57 billion, driven by customer growth and tailored offerings across different segments.
Total mobile subscribers increased 1.6 per cent to 9.93 million, supported by higher postpaid subscriptions.
Consumer home subscriptions rose 2.5 per cent to 807,000, although service revenue edged down 1.2 per cent to RM251 million amid intensified price competition.
Meanwhile, enterprise service revenue climbed 5.3 per cent to RM419 million, driven by growth in internet of things and fixed network solutions.
Overall, Maxis recorded a higher operating free cash flow of RM983 million, supported by strong working capital management.
The group's capital expenditure (capex) stood at RM142 million as it continued to make focused investments in its integrated network infrastructure, including fibre expansion and mobile network capacity upgrades.
Maxis said the quarter's performance reflected continued momentum, with strong execution and operational discipline driving growth across its consumer and enterprise businesses.
The group added that it will continue to offer converged solutions while leveraging digitalisation and artificial intelligence (AI) to enhance customer value and deliver a seamless network experience.
Maxis chief executive officer Goh Seow Eng said the group's first quarter performance shows clear execution of the company's strategy.
"Our disciplined approach continues to yield positive outcomes, translating steady revenue growth into accelerated profitability.
"In recognition of this strong performance, the board declared a dividend of four sen per share, reflecting our continued focus on maintaining a stable dividend payout," he said in a separate statement.
Building on the encouraging start to FY26, Goh said that the group is maintaining its full-year guidance of low single-digit growth in service revenue and earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation, with capex intensity projected at between 10 per cent and 12 per cent.
He said the group remains mindful of macroeconomic volatility and is actively optimising its supply chain and operations to mitigate external headwinds.
"Maxis remains focused on delivering sustainable growth and long-term value for our customers and shareholders," he noted.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: No fuel rationing needed
Article:
SEREMBAN: Malaysia's fuel supply remains sufficient despite global tensions, and there is currently no need for rationing or restrictions on vehicle use, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
He also said Malaysia's position remains stable compared with some neighbouring countries, where fuel stations have reportedly been forced to close amid tightening supply conditions.
"Once again, I emphasise that the country is in a relatively controlled economic position. We know that in a neighbouring country, 400 petrol stations have been closed.
"I just returned from the Asean leaders' summit in Cebu, the Philippines. What was discussed? Fears of insufficient oil supply and diesel shortages," he said in his speech at the National Youth Day 2026 celebration in Sendayan today.
Anwar added, concerns over potential spikes in fuel prices were widely discussed among Asean leaders, driven by geopolitical tensions, including developments in the Strait of Hormuz, as well as disruptions affecting oil- and gas-producing countries.
"This is basic supply and demand theory — prices can surge."
However, the prime minister, who is also finance minister, also said that Malaysia remains in a comfortable position in terms of fuel supply.
"Alhamdulillah, we should be grateful. It does not mean we cannot object or criticise, but we must be thankful.
"This is one of the countries where supply is still sufficient. There is no need for rationing, no need for queues, and no need to restrict vehicle usage."
He added that Malaysians, especially the younger generation, should recognise the importance of the country's energy stability, crediting Petronas' management and government economic policies for ensuring efficiency in the sector.
Anwar also said Malaysia continues to explore new oil and gas opportunities abroad, including through recent overseas missions by Petronas leadership to Africa, Latin America and Turkmenistan.
"Few countries can simply say 'we are from Malaysia' and immediately receive such attention and priority.
"Alhamdulillah, Malaysia is at a level where many countries respect us."
He also urged young people to make full use of existing opportunities, including training and financing facilities provided to them.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Strong fundamentals, reforms to support ringgit; up 3.3pct versus US dollar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's firm economic prospects and sustained reform momentum are expected to provide lasting support for the ringgit moving forward, said Bank Negara Malaysia.
Bank Negara governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said, despite some volatility following the Middle East conflict and reduced expectations for US Federal Reserve policy rate cuts, the ringgit has performed well on a year-to-date basis.
Abdul Rasheed noted that the ringgit has appreciated by 3.3 per cent against the US dollar and 2.9 per cent on a nominal effective exchange rate (NEER) basis as of May 13, 2026.
"Looking ahead, while external factors will continue to drive exchange rate movements, Malaysia's firm economic prospects and sustained reform momentum are expected to provide enduring support to the ringgit.
"Bank Negara will closely monitor global developments and remains committed to ensuring the orderly functioning of the domestic foreign exchange market," he said at a press conference on Malaysia's first quarter (Q1) 2026 gross domestic product (GDP) performance today.
According to Bank Negara, the ringgit strengthened against Malaysia's major trading partners' currencies in Q1 2026, as reflected in the 1.4 per cent NEER appreciation.
The central bank said the local note also appreciated by 0.5 per cent against the US dollar during the quarter, despite the dollar strengthening following the onset of the Middle East conflict amid risk-off sentiment.
The ringgit's appreciation was supported by Malaysia's strong domestic fundamentals and growth momentum, alongside continued non-resident inflows into domestic markets, it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Westports earnings rise 47pct in Q1 to RM326mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Port operator Westports Holdings Bhd posted stronger earnings for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, driven by higher container revenue.
Net profit rose 46.8 per cent to RM326.5 million, compared with RM222.46 million in the same period last year.
Revenue increased 44.2 per cent to RM896.28 million from RM621.29 million previously.
The group's operational revenue came in at RM753 million, up 27 per cent year-on-year, mainly supported by higher container-related revenue.
No dividend was declared for the quarter.
Westports said the Middle East crisis has disrupted global energy supply and driven significant increases in fuel prices.
The group added that fuel costs are expected to rise materially if elevated spot prices persist.
"A sustained high global energy price would also transmit inflationary pressures across global economies, reducing consumers' purchasing power and stressing governments' fiscal positions, thereby necessitating austerity measures that could tilt economies into a recession.
"Container shipping is also altering its port calls and diverting boxes as they avoid sailing through the Strait of Hormuz," Westports said in a filing with Bursa Malaysia.
The port operator said it is still maintaining its single-digit positive container throughput growth for now, despite limited visibility, but will revise its guidance if challenging conditions continue or if volumes decline significantly.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hannah Yeoh: MACC yet to give update on report over shrinking KL retention ponds
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh is awaiting a response from the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) over alleged irregularities tied to the alienation of the Batu and Jinjang flood retention ponds.
Yeoh revealed that she had personally lodged a report on the matter with the anti-graft agency back in 2021.
"Because it is subject to an ongoing investigation, I have to get an official status update from the MACC before I can comment further," she told reporters after attending the launch of the KTM Segambut Utara station in Kuala Lumpur.
She added that her office has repeatedly followed up with the commission but has yet to receive a response.
"We asked but didn't get a response. So we will continue to follow up."
The minister was responding to comments by former Federal Territories Minister Datuk Seri
Tengku Adnan Tengku Mansoor
, who denied any knowledge of flood retention pond land transfers to developers during his tenure, claiming land matters were outside his ministry's purview at the time.
Earlier this week, Yeoh said that the capacity of the critical flood retention ponds in Jinjang and Batu had
shrunk by an estimated 70 per cent
after 17 land lots within the water body zones were transferred to developers starting from 2015.
The shrinkage severely compromised the original 33.5ha flood mitigation system, which was introduced in the early 2000s to curb flash floods in the capital.
Following the revelation, Yeoh ordered an immediate freeze on all development permissions for the remaining lots.
When asked what safeguards are being put in place to prevent future incidents, Yeoh said a dedicated task force under the Federal Territories Department is now working closely with the Department of Irrigation and Drainage (DID) and Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL).
She added that moving forward, any development order or application submitted to DBKL must fully satisfy the stringent technical requirements set out by DID before gaining approval.
Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud confirmed that guidelines have been tightened.
"We are currently checking the finer details with the landowners and reviewing pond maintenance. We have also added additional guidelines strictly for management and operational purposes," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Workplaces not safe if employees face burnout, harrassment: Lee Lam Thye
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Workplaces cannot be considered safe if employees are suffering from burnout due to unrealistic workloads or are subjected to bullying and harassment, according to Mental Health Advisory Council member Lee Lam Thye.
He said the Employment Hero's Wellness at Work Report for 2024 and 2022 showed an alarming burnout rate among Malaysian employees, with 67 per cent of workers experiencing burnout in 2024, a significant jump from 58 per cent in 2022.
"These are not 'soft issues'. These are psychosocial risks - and they are just as dangerous as a slippery floor or faulty machinery. In fact, their impact can be even more devastating because they affect the mind.
"Just as we recognise the importance of physical health in ensuring that employees are able to perform their duties safely and effectively, we must now acknowledge that mental health is equally - if not more - critical in shaping productivity, decision-making, relationships, and overall organisational success," he said in a statement today.
He said that mental health also underpins physical health, with chronic stress closely linked to serious conditions such as heart disease, hypertension, weakened immunity and sleep disorders.
Lee said that while productivity is often measured through absenteeism, or employees not coming to work, a more dangerous issue is presenteeism, where employees are physically present but mentally absent.
"They sit at their desks, but their minds are burdened by stress, anxiety, or depression. They cannot focus. They cannot perform. And sometimes they make critical mistakes.
"This is not just a human issue; it is an economic issue. Presenteeism quietly drains productivity and impacts our national growth. If we ignore mental health, we are not only failing our people, we are failing our economy," he stressed.
He said employers must recognise that creating a safe and supportive psychosocial work environment is a responsibility, not an option.
This includes promoting open conversations on mental health without stigma or discrimination; providing access to mental health resources, counselling, and employee assistance programmes; training managers to recognise early signs of mental distress and respond with empathy; ensuring reasonable workloads, work-life balance, and fair treatment at all levels; and establishing clear policies to address workplace bullying, harassment, and toxic cultures.
Lee also called on the government to recognise psychosocial risks within the national occupational safety and health (OSH) framework, with policies that treat psychological safety as seriously as physical safety.
"The reality we can no longer ignore today is that we do not just bring our skills to work, we bring our whole selves, including our anxieties, our depressions, and our psychological burdens. Breaking the silence on mental health is not just a trend; it is a modern workplace necessity.
"No one should have to suffer in silence. It is time we acknowledge that mental health matters—at every workplace, at every level, and for every individual," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia lower at midday, tracking regional peers amid US-China Summit watch
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended the morning trading session lower, tracking weaker regional market performance as investors remained cautious and kept a close watch on developments from the US-China Summit.
At 12.30 pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) eased 6.11 points, or 0.35 per cent, to 1,739.47 from Thursday's close of 1,745.58.
The benchmark index, which opened 2.11 points higher at 1,747.69, moved between 1,735.51 and 1,748.85 during the session.
However, market breadth was negative with losers outnumbering gainers 713 to 361, while 535 counters were unchanged, 1,119 untraded and 47 suspended.
Turnover stood at 2.36 billion units valued at RM1.65 billion.
More to come
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teachers brave lake journeys to educate Orang Asli children at floating school
Article:
GERIK: Deep in the remote interior of Perak, where internet access is scarce and journeys require long boat rides across Tasik Banding, two teachers are redefining the meaning of sacrifice in education.
For Syed Nafiz Muqri Danial Syed Ahmad Ezahar, 27, and Muhammad Aizuddin Asmadi Afandi, 26, serving at the country's first Tasik Banding floating school is not merely about teaching in a classroom, but ensuring Orang Asli children are not left behind in education.
They are among teachers from Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) RPS Kemar K11 who take turns teaching on the floating boats from Monday to Wednesday.
The school, located within the Temiar and Jahai Orang Asli settlement, caters to children who previously struggled to attend schools outside the area due to long and difficult travel routes.
Ahead of Teachers' Day celebrations, Syed Nafiz, from Sungai Petani, Kedah, said the experience had changed his perspective about the teaching profession.
"In places like this, you learn that teaching is not about comfort. It is about sacrifice, patience and dedication," he said.
He added that teachers assigned to the floating school are required to prepare teaching materials, food supplies and other necessities before entering the settlement due to the poor internet connectivity in the area.
"We have to prepare everything before coming in because there is basically no internet here," he said.
Despite the challenges, Syed Nafiz said he has grown deeply attached to the children and enjoys even the simplest classroom activities.
"Most of the students here are still very young and I genuinely enjoy teaching them. Even teaching simple things like the ABCs becomes enjoyable," he said.
One of the biggest challenges he initially faced was overcoming the language barrier, as many students primarily communicate in the Temiar and Jahai dialects.
"When I first came here, I struggled because I had never heard the language before. But slowly, I learned how to communicate with them and it became easier," he said.
Syed Nafiz admitted he initially worried whether the children would be able to follow the lessons or recognise letters, but was surprised by their eagerness to learn.
[[nid:1440160]]
"They already know many basic things. Their weakness is mainly spelling, but the understanding is already there," he said.
Meanwhile, Muhammad Aizuddin, from Pahang, described the journey to the school itself as among the biggest challenges faced by teachers stationed there.
Teachers are required to travel up to 30 minutes by road from Gerik town to the Pulau Banding jetty before continuing another hour by boat to reach the school.
"If you want to say it is difficult, then yes, it is difficult. We travel from town to the jetty and then continue by boat for almost another hour before arriving at the floating school," he said.
He said unreliable internet coverage, water supply disruptions and occasional electricity outages were among the difficulties teachers had to adapt to while serving in the remote settlement.
"The school operates on a rotation system where teachers are assigned every week, allowing students to interact with different educators regularly," he said.
Muhammad Aizuddin said many children in the settlement rarely meet outsiders, making the arrival of new teachers an exciting experience for them.
[[nid:1440159]]
"Every school has its own atmosphere and challenges. The sacrifices teachers make are different everywhere, but teachers do not mind as long as their students succeed," he said.
In the event of a death in the family or an accident, he said affected families would first inform the school administration, which would then coordinate with teachers who own boats to arrange transportation to the remote settlement and the floating school.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: CGC joins Bank Negara's Stabilisation Relief Facility to help SMEs amid Middle East conflict
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Credit Guarantee Corp Malaysia Bhd (CGC) has participated in Bank Negara Malaysia's effort to assist small and medium enterprises (SMEs) experiencing cash flow pressures amid the ongoing economic uncertainty through the SME Stabilisation Relief Facility.
The facility allows eligible SMEs to obtain working capital financing of up to RM750,000 for a tenure of up to five years. The financing rate is capped at 3.75 per cent per annum, inclusive of a guarantee fee of 0.5 per cent per annum, to ensure financing remains accessible to all SMEs.
It will be made available through participating financial institutions.
CGC Malaysia chairman Datuk Mohammed Hussein said CGC is privileged to play a central role in assisting the SMEs.
"It is a strong reflection of the maturity and responsiveness of our financial services sector, and the strong collaboration between the Finance Ministry, Bank Negara and our participating financial institutions that we are able to produce a comprehensive stabilisation package within just two months of the onset of the Middle East conflict.
"Our mandate has long been to keep credit flowing to viable businesses through periods of stress, and the facility is precisely the kind of counter-cyclical intervention CGC is mandated to perform," he said.
The facility is open to a broad range of business entities, including sole proprietorships, partnerships, limited liability partnerships, private limited companies, and professional practices, provided they are locally incorporated with a minimum of 51 per cent Malaysian ownership.
In addition, SMEs that are anticipating or are already experiencing financial pressures are encouraged to engage early with their respective financial institutions.
"CGC's role under this facility is to work hand-in-hand with participating financial institutions to ensure that otherwise viable SMEs are not constrained by short-term liquidity pressures.
"By taking on a substantial share of credit risk through our guarantee, we enable financial institutions to extend working capital financing more efficiently and at concessionary rates, allowing affected businesses to sustain operations and safeguard jobs with minimal disruption," said CGC president and chief executive officer Mohamed Nazri Omar.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jho Low pardon bid: Anwar says ‘we are not going that route’, leaves US decision to Washington
Article:
SEREMBAN, May 15 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Malaysia will not consider any pardon application involving fugitive businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low.
He said the matter was a non-issue for the Malaysian Government as court proceedings involving the individual were still ongoing.
“It’s a non-issue. We are not going to consider that. No,” he told reporters after officiating the 2026 National Youth Day (HBN) celebration here today.
Asked whether the government would formally oppose any move by the United States (US) regarding the matter, Anwar said: “As far as we are concerned, we are not going that route...but of course, what the US decides is their own business.”
The Prime Minister also said that he was unaware of any pardon application submitted by Jho Low.
“We have made it very clear, it’s a non-issue. Even if he has submitted an application (for pardon), it’s not an issue that we should discuss because he is still undergoing this process in court,” he said.
Two days ago, foreign media reported that Jho Low, who has been accused by the US of masterminding one of the largest financial frauds in history, had allegedly filed a pardon application to US President Donald Trump several weeks ago.
According to the report, approval of the application would result in the US criminal charges against Jho Low being dropped.
The report also stated that the US Department of Justice website listed an application filed under the name Taek Jho Low this year as “pending” for a “Pardon after Completion of Sentence”.
The businessman was charged in 2018 with misappropriating about US$4.5 billion (RM17.7 billion) from 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB) funds, but remains at large and wanted by authorities. — Bernama

Title: On national Youth Day, Anwar announces RM5m allocation for Youth Council
Article:
SEREMBAN, May 15 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced a RM5 million allocation for the Malaysian Youth Council (MBM) to strengthen the umbrella body’s role in advancing youth development in the country.
He said the additional RM1 million allocation compared with last year was recognition of MBM’s role as the main driver of the national youth agenda under the Ministry of Youth and Sports (KBS), while reflecting the government’s continued commitment to supporting youth development programmes.
“MBM is the sole youth association under the KBS. So this year, I agree to increase it to RM5 million… last year it was RM4 million,” he said when officiating the 2026 National Youth Day celebration here.
Also present were Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun and Youth and Sports Minister Dr Mohammed Taufiq Johari.
Meanwhile, Anwar said the government had also allocated funds under the Islamic Development Programme benefiting 7,091 Kelas Al-Quran dan Fardu Ain (Kafa) teachers, imams, takmir teachers and mosque personnel nationwide, involving a total financial commitment of RM42.3 million.
The Prime Minister said support would also extend to Islamic educational institutions such as pondok schools, tahfiz centres, religious schools and Kafa premises operating outside the supervision of the Education Ministry, with RM5.4 million allocated for maintenance and institutional strengthening.
He said three projects in Negeri Sembilan had been approved by the Prime Minister’s Department Implementation Coordination Unit (ICU), involving RM800,000 in allocations for minor repair works at Maahad Tahfiz Al-Quran Al-Falah Kuala Sawah Rantau, Madrasah Az-Zaakiriin and Pondok Pesantren Darul Tauhid.
In his speech earlier, the Prime Minister also praised Aminuddin’s resilience and determination in navigating the state’s prolonged political challenges while continuing to prioritise the people’s welfare.
Anwar said he appreciated Aminuddin’s steadfast leadership and focus on developing the state despite facing continuous political pressures.
“I salute Menteri Besar Aminuddin for the resilience and spirit he has shown in facing the state’s never-ending political turmoil,” he said. — Bernama

Title: New MACC chief Halim Aman vows stronger cooperation with AGC on high-profile cases
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 15 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) will continue strengthening its strategic cooperation with the Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC), particularly in handling high-profile, large-scale and public interest cases.
Newly-appointed MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said such cases would continue to be referred to the AGC as the authority responsible for determining legal action on investigations conducted by the anti-graft agency.
“I will do my best to lead, reform and strengthen the MACC,” he said in a statement today following a courtesy visit to Attorney-General Tan Sri Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar yesterday.
The visit marked Abd Halim’s first official engagement since assuming office on May 13.
Meanwhile, Mohd Dusuki expressed appreciation for Abd Halim’s decision to make the AGC his first courtesy visit as the new MACC chief commissioner.
“The AGC and the MACC are inseparable. The cooperation between us will continue,” he said.
Also present were the head of the AGC’s Trial and Appeals Division, Datuk Seri Saiful Edris Zainuddin, and the head of the AGC’s Prosecution Division, Datuk Masri Mohd Daud. — Bernama

Title: Anwar: Jho Low’s US pardon application still subject to court process
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — Malaysia will not oppose a bid by a fugitive businessman involved in the massive 1MDB corruption scandal to seek a pardon from US President Donald Trump, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today.
Speaking to reporters during a visit to Seremban district, south of Kuala Lumpur, Anwar described the plea by Low Taek Jho as a “non-issue”.
The businessman, better known as Jho Low, is formally seeking a “pardon after completion of sentence”, according to the US Department of Justice website.
Whistleblowers allege that Jho Low, a well-connected Malaysian financier with no official role, helped set up the 1MDB state investment fund and made key financial decisions before disappearing about a decade ago.
Low, who has been indicted in the United States, has denied wrongdoing but remains at large.
“As far as we are concerned, we are not going down that route,” Anwar said when asked if the government would formally oppose Jho Low’s plea.
“Even if he has submitted, it is not an issue that we should discuss because he is still undergoing this process in the courts.”
Malaysia’s Trade Minister Johari Abdul Ghani, who chairs a task force seeking to recover assets linked to 1MDB worldwide, said Wednesday the plea should be rejected, and Low returned for trial.
The 1MDB fund was launched by former prime minister Najib Razak in 2009, shortly after he became premier.
It is alleged that more than US$4.5 billion was diverted from 1MDB between 2009 and 2015 by fund officials and associates, including Low.
Najib, who has been convicted in multiple cases, has been jailed and fined US$2.8 billion for his role in the plunder.
Najib’s defence lawyers blamed Low and dubbed him the mastermind of the scheme.
Malaysia unsuccessfully sought the return of Low through extradition, and it was widely speculated in the media that he was hiding in China.
The scandal shook Malaysian politics, contributing to the 2018 downfall of the ruling coalition that had governed since independence in 1957, and led to the convictions of two former Goldman Sachs bankers.
Investigators said top officials used their ill-gotten gains to splurge on assets worldwide, including a luxury yacht, high-end real estate, Monet as well as Van Gogh paintings, and even to fund the Hollywood blockbuster “The Wolf of Wall Street”.
Actor Leonardo DiCaprio testified in court about Low’s wild spending sprees and lavish parties. — AFP

Title: Anthony Loke labels backlash against UEC public university access an attempt to politicise education
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 15 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke today slammed criticism over the move to open public universities to graduates of Chinese Independent High Schools (SMPC) and Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) holders, describing it as an attempt to politicise education.
Yesterday, the Higher Education Ministry (KPT) announced that students from outside the national education system, including UEC holders, would be allowed to enrol in public universities, marking a watershed decision as the UEC system has long been a flashpoint in race relations.
Some groups alleged that the move promotes Chinese chauvinism, an accusation UEC advocates have described as baseless.
“I find it strange that some parties are protesting this government commitment. They are opposing people’s access to education,” Loke said at a press conference here.
“In fact, we want to increase educational access so that we can educate these children to receive a better education. If they oppose this, it shows that they are denying our children’s right to study at a higher level,” he added.
“This is a government commitment, just as announced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim last January.”
Shortly after the announcement, PAS information chief Ahmad Fadhli Shaari reportedly claimed that opening opportunities for UEC students to pursue studies at public universities (IPTA) was a “magic trick” tactic by the government to expand educational access for students from that stream.
He claimed that combining tahfiz graduates and UEC holders under the same announcement was a subtle attempt to legitimise the admission of UEC students into public universities.
Under the new framework, students from these streams who possess a full Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) certificate may apply for any study programme offered through the standard UPUOnline system.
Their applications will be considered based on merit alongside other candidates, subject to meeting the general and specific programme requirements.
The second pathway involves applicants without a full SPM certificate but who have passed the Bahasa Melayu and History papers. The ministry said a new pathway has been created for them to apply for a specific list of relevant degree and diploma programmes.
For religious and tahfiz school graduates under this second pathway, the identified programmes include studies in Dakwah (Islamic Outreach), Al-Quran and As-Sunnah, and Tahfiz Education.

Title: Tiger teeth, bear fangs among RM5.32m wildlife parts seized in Kota Kinabalu raid
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, May 15 — Sabah wildlife authorities believe they have uncovered an illegal wildlife parts trading operation in the heart of the city following a raid on an antique shop along Jalan Gaya here two days ago.
In a statement today, Sabah Wildlife Department director Soffian Abu Bakar said the raid uncovered several wildlife parts from protected species, including 16 tusks believed to be from wild boars, nine teeth believed to be from tigers, 10 teeth suspected to be from bears, various edible bird’s nest products of different weights, as well as 13 stones believed to be from porcupines.
A 52-year-old local man, believed to be the owner of the premises, Antique Shop Sdn Bhd, was detained to assist investigations into possession of the illegal items.
The operation was carried out jointly with the Royal Malaysia Police through Bukit Aman’s Wildlife Crime Bureau and Special Investigation Intelligence (WCB/PSK) unit following intelligence gathering and surveillance.
WCB/PSK commander Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar was previously reported as saying the total estimated value of the seized items was RM5.32 million.
According to him, preliminary interviews found that the suspect admitted purchasing the bird’s nests and porcupine stones from suppliers in the Lintas area without valid licences or permits.
“The protected wildlife parts are believed to have been obtained from villagers in Tawau at low prices before being resold in the local market around Kota Kinabalu.
“All the items did not have any storage licences or permits from the authorities,” he said.
The case is being investigated under Sections 41(1) and 41(2) of the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997 for allegedly possessing, storing and displaying wildlife parts without valid permits.
